positive.comment: Congrats on the launch! Couple tips on App Store Optimization that will help with growth: Utilize all the space in your app name. These keywords hold the most weight and Apple will prioritize them when formulating keyword phrases Create organic keyword phrases that normal people would use. Nothing against your current subtitle, but my assumption is no one is searching for any of those words except for support. Use a keyword analysis tool like Apptweak to get a sense on the right keywords to target. Caption your screenshots. You don't need anything fancy, but it will help potential users understand the core value promise that you are delivering on. Also, double dipping on keywords you use in your title and subtitle will further compound their impact. Best of luck out there! And keep launching :)

Amazing, thanks for your honest feedback, keep us posted on how you do. In terms of getting it out there, I would recommend posting your product to as many A-listers and celebs on TikTok, Instagram, and Twitter (can't use the word X). A free plug from a space with a huge following will help get your product to increase usage.
positive.parent: Hey thanks, appreciate the curiosity! I built Comforto using + Supabase for the backend and auth, moshi for audio agents and llm model training. On the app side, it's a React Native / Expo build (so I could ship to both iOS and Android later), with a light Firebase layer for analytics and push. Costs are actually minimal right now: • Hosting + Supabase → under $20/month • voice agents- upto $3 for 30 minutes of interaction. • Everything else (frontend hosting, analytics, etc.) is free or hobby-tier.

positive.comment: That's a good question actually! I wanted to go with real phone calls because the fake call screen trick doesn't always hold up in real situations — people can notice the screen or timing feels off. A real call gives you that authentic ring tone, vibration, and interaction with your carrier's call UI — it just feels real. Plus, since it's an actual voice line, you can pick up, talk, and it sounds completely natural to anyone nearby. So yeah, the goal was to make it feel believable, not staged.
